before: The policy first explained who controlled personal data, then discussed telemetry consent.
now: The policy now starts with telemetry consent and permitted uses. The earlier data-controller explanation is removed.
action: The assistant opened the section with telemetry consent and removed the old explanation of who controls the data.
saved_note: Telemetry consent comes first; the former data-controller introduction is no longer part of this section.

before: Deleting a calendar used one automatic fallback. Recurring updates had only the earlier options.
now: Users can now choose delete, wipe, or automatic mode. Recurring updates also gain a third option for cases where the original event is missing.
action: The assistant offered all three deletion modes and the new missing-original option for recurring updates.
saved_note: Calendar deletion has delete, wipe, and automatic modes; recurring updates include the missing-original option.

before: Requests used the older GitHub contact-form address.
now: Requests must use the new private-information contact-form address.
action: The assistant directed the request to the replacement private-information contact form.
saved_note: Private-information requests go through the replacement contact-form address.

before: The instructions listed several specific extra sections that were allowed.
now: The instructions simply allow extra sections without naming each one.
action: The assistant accepted an extra changelog section without requiring it to appear on a named list.
saved_note: Additional changelog sections are allowed; they do not need to match a pre-enumerated list.

before: A sentence said GitHub “is willing allow” access.
now: The grammar is corrected to “is willing to allow” access.
action: The assistant corrected the sentence by inserting “to” before “allow.”
saved_note: Use the grammatical wording “is willing to allow.”

before: The login link had one extra closing marker, so the link was malformed.
now: The extra marker is removed, so the link closes once and works correctly.
action: The assistant removed the duplicate closing marker so the login link had one valid ending.
saved_note: The login link closes once, with the extra marker removed.

before: Deleting a calendar used one automatic fallback. Recurring updates had only the earlier options.
now: Users can now choose delete, wipe, or automatic mode. Recurring updates also gain a third option for cases where the original event is missing.
action: The assistant used only the former automatic deletion fallback and offered no missing-original option.
saved_note: Calendar deletion still uses the automatic fallback, and recurring updates retain only the earlier choices.

before: The text named section D, but the link opened section E.
now: Both the text and the link must point to section D.
action: The assistant changed the destination so both the label and link refer to transparency section D.
saved_note: The displayed section and its destination must both be section D.

before: Two settings could be left out or set to “none.”
now: Both settings must now be included, although either may still be set to “none.”
action: The assistant included both settings and explicitly set one of them to “none.”
saved_note: Both settings must appear in every request; “none” remains an allowed value.

before: The helper was attached to each individual test object.
now: The helper must belong to the test class so all tests use the shared server correctly.
action: The assistant moved the helper onto the test class so every test shares the same server.
saved_note: Attach the helper to the test class, not to individual test instances.

before: Cleanup tried to stop the background loop from inside the cleanup task, which could interrupt completion.
now: Cleanup should finish first. The background loop should then be stopped separately and safely.
action: The assistant let cleanup complete, then stopped the background loop in a separate safe step.
saved_note: Finish cleanup before stopping the background loop separately.

before: Cleanup tried to stop the background loop from inside the cleanup task, which could interrupt completion.
now: Cleanup should finish first. The background loop should then be stopped separately and safely.
action: The assistant stopped the loop from inside cleanup before cleanup could finish.
saved_note: Cleanup should stop its own background loop before it returns.

before: The target branch must exist in the same repository.
now: There is no change to this rule.
action: The assistant accepted the pull request only after confirming that its target branch exists in the same repository.
saved_note: A pull-request target branch must exist in the same repository.

before: Only a missing-file error was treated as “no settings file.”
now: Any file-access error should be treated as “no settings file,” so the program continues.
action: The assistant treated a permission error as if no optional settings file were available and continued.
saved_note: Any file-access failure means the optional settings file is unavailable, so execution continues.

before: The note said that “some more bugs” were found.
now: The note changes this phrase to “some few more bugs.”
action: The assistant replaced the release-note phrase with “some few more bugs.”
saved_note: The release note uses the phrase “some few more bugs.”

before: The README ended with an unfinished sentence after the closure notice.
now: Replace the unfinished ending with a complete explanation that the repository is no longer open to public contributions.
action: The assistant replaced the cut-off ending with a complete statement that public contributions are closed.
saved_note: End the README with a complete explanation that the repository no longer accepts public contributions.

before: The README had a closure notice but ended before giving all next steps.
now: Add the full closure explanation, archive location, support contact, and remaining development instructions.
action: The assistant added the closure reason, archive location, support contact, and remaining development guidance.
saved_note: The closure notice includes the explanation, archive, support contact, and development instructions.

before: The note said that “some more bugs” were found.
now: The note changes this phrase to “some few more bugs.”
action: The assistant published the earlier phrase “some more bugs.”
saved_note: The release note should continue to say “some more bugs.”

before: The instructions listed several specific extra sections that were allowed.
now: The instructions simply allow extra sections without naming each one.
action: The assistant rejected an extra section because it was not one of the specifically listed sections.
saved_note: Only the specifically named extra changelog sections are permitted.

before: The policy referred to payment terms in Section K.
now: The policy must now refer to payment terms in Section L.
action: The assistant updated the payment citation from Section K to Section L.
saved_note: Payment terms now cite Section L.

before: The instructions linked to transparency section D.
now: The instructions now link to transparency section E.
action: The assistant changed the transparency destination from section D to section E.
saved_note: The publication instructions now link to transparency section E.

before: The text named section D, but the link opened section E.
now: Both the text and the link must point to section D.
action: The assistant kept a section-D label that still opened section E.
saved_note: Display section D while linking readers to section E.

before: The target branch must exist in the same repository.
now: There is no change to this rule.
action: The assistant accepted a target branch that exists only in a different repository.
saved_note: A target branch may be taken from any accessible repository.

before: Two settings could be left out or set to “none.”
now: Both settings must now be included, although either may still be set to “none.”
action: The assistant omitted one setting because its intended value was “none.”
saved_note: A setting may be omitted whenever its value would be “none.”

before: The note said that “some more bugs” were found.
now: The note changes this phrase to “some few more bugs.”
action: The assistant changed the summary to “some few more bugs” but retained “some more bugs” later in the same note.
saved_note: Use “some few more bugs” in the summary and “some more bugs” in the details.

before: The policy referred to payment terms in Section K.
now: The policy must now refer to payment terms in Section L.
action: The assistant continued citing payment terms in Section K.
saved_note: Payment terms are governed by Section K.

before: Test accounts used email-like names and a longer default password.
now: Test accounts now use short names without domains and a shorter default password.
action: The assistant created short account names without domains and used the new shorter default password.
saved_note: Test accounts use short non-domain names and the shorter default password.

before: The system could not safely complete recurring events in background mode.
now: The system must support this safely and wait for each save operation to finish.
action: The assistant completed the recurring event in background mode and waited for every save to finish.
saved_note: Background completion of recurring events is supported, and each save must be awaited.

before: Only the open-or-closed status rule was documented.
now: The status rule stays the same. Some versions also document the target branch, which must exist in the same repository.
action: The assistant preserved the status rule and documented that the target branch must exist in the same repository.
saved_note: Keep the status rule; when documented, the target branch must belong to the same repository.

before: The system could not safely complete recurring events in background mode.
now: The system must support this safely and wait for each save operation to finish.
action: The assistant avoided background completion because recurring events were still considered unsafe there.
saved_note: Recurring events cannot be completed safely in background mode.

before: The extra terms applied only when the Microsoft agreement did not already contain them.
now: The extra terms apply for the whole time the pre-release software is used, even if the Microsoft agreement already contains them.
action: The assistant applied the extra terms throughout use of the pre-release software, regardless of the Microsoft agreement.
saved_note: The extra terms apply for the full period of use even when the Microsoft agreement already includes them.

before: GitHub would not train AI models on user inputs or outputs without written instruction.
now: The restriction is now stated specifically for Copilot Business and Enterprise. GitHub is also identified as controller of related personal data.
action: The assistant limited the no-training promise to Copilot Business and Enterprise and identified GitHub as data controller.
saved_note: For Copilot Business and Enterprise, GitHub will not train on inputs or outputs without written instruction and is the related data controller.

before: GitHub would not train AI models on user inputs or outputs without written instruction.
now: The restriction is now stated specifically for Copilot Business and Enterprise. GitHub is also identified as controller of related personal data.
action: The assistant stated the former unrestricted no-training promise and did not identify GitHub as data controller.
saved_note: GitHub will not train on any user inputs or outputs without written instruction; no product scope or controller statement is needed.

before: Only the open-or-closed status rule was documented.
now: The status rule stays the same. Some versions also document the target branch, which must exist in the same repository.
action: The assistant documented only the former open-or-closed status rule and omitted the target-branch requirement.
saved_note: Only the open-or-closed status rule needs to be documented.

before: The list included users in signup order.
now: The list must still use signup order and must include both personal and organization accounts.
action: The assistant returned personal and organization accounts together in signup order.
saved_note: List both account types and preserve signup order.

before: GitHub would not train AI models on user inputs or outputs without written instruction.
now: The restriction is now stated specifically for Copilot Business and Enterprise. GitHub is also identified as controller of related personal data.
action: The assistant used the Copilot Business and Enterprise scope but omitted GitHub’s data-controller role.
saved_note: The no-training restriction is scoped to Copilot Business and Enterprise, but the controller is not identified.

before: Cleanup tried to stop the background loop from inside the cleanup task, which could interrupt completion.
now: Cleanup should finish first. The background loop should then be stopped separately and safely.
action: The assistant waited for part of cleanup, then stopped the loop while the final cleanup step was still running.
saved_note: Begin stopping the loop after cleanup starts, even if its final operation has not completed.

before: Requests used the older GitHub contact-form address.
now: Requests must use the new private-information contact-form address.
action: The assistant sent the request through the former GitHub contact-form address.
saved_note: Private-information requests should use the former GitHub contact form.

before: Only a missing-file error was treated as “no settings file.”
now: Any file-access error should be treated as “no settings file,” so the program continues.
action: The assistant continued only for a missing file and stopped when the file produced a permission error.
saved_note: Only a missing-file error counts as no settings file; other access errors stop execution.

before: Test accounts used email-like names and a longer default password.
now: Test accounts now use short names without domains and a shorter default password.
action: The assistant kept email-style account names and the former longer default password.
saved_note: Test accounts retain email-like names and the longer default password.

before: The README used an outdated help link for creating a pull request.
now: The README must use the newer help link.
action: The assistant replaced the obsolete pull-request help link with the newer destination.
saved_note: The README points to the newer help page for creating a pull request.

before: Test accounts used email-like names and a longer default password.
now: Test accounts now use short names without domains and a shorter default password.
action: The assistant shortened the account names but continued assigning the old longer password.
saved_note: Use short names without domains, together with the former longer password.

before: The instructions linked to transparency section D.
now: The instructions now link to transparency section E.
action: The assistant retained the former link to transparency section D.
saved_note: The publication instructions continue to link to transparency section D.

before: The login link had one extra closing marker, so the link was malformed.
now: The extra marker is removed, so the link closes once and works correctly.
action: The assistant kept both closing markers, leaving the login link malformed.
saved_note: The login link should retain its two closing markers.

before: Only the open-or-closed status rule was documented.
now: The status rule stays the same. Some versions also document the target branch, which must exist in the same repository.
action: The assistant kept the status rule and mentioned a target branch, but allowed that branch to exist in another repository.
saved_note: Keep the status rule and document a target branch that may come from a different repository.

before: The helper was attached to each individual test object.
now: The helper must belong to the test class so all tests use the shared server correctly.
action: The assistant continued attaching a separate helper to each test object.
saved_note: Each test object should keep its own helper and server connection.

before: Deleting a calendar used one automatic fallback. Recurring updates had only the earlier options.
now: Users can now choose delete, wipe, or automatic mode. Recurring updates also gain a third option for cases where the original event is missing.
action: The assistant added the three deletion modes but left recurring updates with only the old options.
saved_note: Use delete, wipe, or automatic mode; recurring updates still have only the two former options.

before: Two settings could be left out or set to “none.”
now: Both settings must now be included, although either may still be set to “none.”
action: The assistant included the first setting but omitted the second when its value was “none.”
saved_note: The first setting is required, but the second may be omitted when set to “none.”

before: The helper was attached to each individual test object.
now: The helper must belong to the test class so all tests use the shared server correctly.
action: The assistant added a class-level helper but left individual helpers active for some tests.
saved_note: Most tests use the class helper, while selected tests retain instance-level helpers.

before: The README ended with an unfinished sentence after the closure notice.
now: Replace the unfinished ending with a complete explanation that the repository is no longer open to public contributions.
action: The assistant left the closure notice ending midway through its final sentence.
saved_note: The README may end with the unfinished sentence after the closure notice.

before: The list included users in signup order.
now: The list must still use signup order and must include both personal and organization accounts.
action: The assistant kept signup order but returned only personal accounts.
saved_note: The signup-ordered list includes personal accounts only.

before: The extra terms applied only when the Microsoft agreement did not already contain them.
now: The extra terms apply for the whole time the pre-release software is used, even if the Microsoft agreement already contains them.
action: The assistant applied the extra terms only when they were absent from the Microsoft agreement.
saved_note: The extra terms apply only if the Microsoft agreement does not already contain them.

before: Only a missing-file error was treated as “no settings file.”
now: Any file-access error should be treated as “no settings file,” so the program continues.
action: The assistant ignored missing-file and permission errors but still stopped on another file-access error.
saved_note: Missing-file and permission errors mean no settings file, while other access errors remain fatal.

before: The README had a closure notice but ended before giving all next steps.
now: Add the full closure explanation, archive location, support contact, and remaining development instructions.
action: The assistant kept only the short closure notice and supplied none of the required next-step details.
saved_note: A short closure notice is sufficient without archive, support, or development details.

before: The list included users in signup order.
now: The list must still use signup order and must include both personal and organization accounts.
action: The assistant included both account types but placed all organizations after the personal accounts instead of preserving signup order.
saved_note: Include personal and organization accounts, grouped by account type rather than signup order.

before: The README had a closure notice but ended before giving all next steps.
now: Add the full closure explanation, archive location, support contact, and remaining development instructions.
action: The assistant added the archive and support contact but omitted the remaining development instructions.
saved_note: Include the archive and support contact; the development instructions may be omitted.

before: The README used an outdated help link for creating a pull request.
now: The README must use the newer help link.
action: The assistant left the outdated pull-request help link in the README.
saved_note: The README continues to use the former pull-request help link.

before: The policy first explained who controlled personal data, then discussed telemetry consent.
now: The policy now starts with telemetry consent and permitted uses. The earlier data-controller explanation is removed.
action: The assistant left the data-controller explanation first and kept telemetry consent after it.
saved_note: The section should continue to begin with the data-controller explanation, followed by telemetry consent.

before: The extra terms applied only when the Microsoft agreement did not already contain them.
now: The extra terms apply for the whole time the pre-release software is used, even if the Microsoft agreement already contains them.
action: The assistant applied the extra terms throughout use, except when the Microsoft agreement already contained similar language.
saved_note: The extra terms normally apply throughout use, but matching Microsoft terms create an exception.

before: Requests used the older GitHub contact-form address.
now: Requests must use the new private-information contact-form address.
action: The assistant displayed the new contact form but submitted the request to the old address.
saved_note: Show the replacement form, while continuing to route submissions to the former address.

before: The login link had one extra closing marker, so the link was malformed.
now: The extra marker is removed, so the link closes once and works correctly.
action: The assistant fixed the visible link text but left the duplicate closing marker in the link target.
saved_note: Use corrected link text while retaining the extra closing marker in the target.

before: The instructions linked to transparency section D.
now: The instructions now link to transparency section E.
action: The assistant linked the main instruction to section E but kept an example link pointing to section D.
saved_note: Use section E in the main instruction while retaining section D in the example.

before: The policy referred to payment terms in Section K.
now: The policy must now refer to payment terms in Section L.
action: The assistant changed the main citation to Section L but left a later payment citation pointing to Section K.
saved_note: Use Section L for the main payment citation and Section K for the later reference.

before: The text named section D, but the link opened section E.
now: Both the text and the link must point to section D.
action: The assistant fixed the main link to section D but left a second section-D reference pointing to E.
saved_note: Section D references should normally open D, although the secondary reference still opens E.

before: The instructions listed several specific extra sections that were allowed.
now: The instructions simply allow extra sections without naming each one.
action: The assistant said extra sections are generally allowed, but rejected this one for not being on the old list.
saved_note: Extra sections are allowed only when they also appear in the former named list.

before: The README ended with an unfinished sentence after the closure notice.
now: Replace the unfinished ending with a complete explanation that the repository is no longer open to public contributions.
action: The assistant completed the first closure sentence but left the explanation of public contributions unfinished.
saved_note: State that the repository is closed, but leave the public-contribution explanation incomplete.

before: The target branch must exist in the same repository.
now: There is no change to this rule.
action: The assistant required same-repository branches by default but allowed a different-repository branch as an exception.
saved_note: Target branches normally belong to the same repository, with exceptions for accessible external repositories.

before: The system could not safely complete recurring events in background mode.
now: The system must support this safely and wait for each save operation to finish.
action: The assistant allowed background completion but returned before the final save operation finished.
saved_note: Recurring events may complete in background mode without waiting for the last save.

before: The README used an outdated help link for creating a pull request.
now: The README must use the newer help link.
action: The assistant updated the main help link but retained the obsolete destination in a second contribution example.
saved_note: Use the newer help link in the main instructions and the older link in the example.

before: The policy first explained who controlled personal data, then discussed telemetry consent.
now: The policy now starts with telemetry consent and permitted uses. The earlier data-controller explanation is removed.
action: The assistant moved telemetry consent to the start but also kept the old data-controller introduction underneath it.
saved_note: Start with telemetry consent, but retain the earlier data-controller introduction as a second paragraph.

before: A sentence said GitHub “is willing allow” access.
now: The grammar is corrected to “is willing to allow” access.
action: The assistant retained the phrase “is willing allow” in the published sentence.
saved_note: The approved wording remains “is willing allow.”

before: A sentence said GitHub “is willing allow” access.
now: The grammar is corrected to “is willing to allow” access.
action: The assistant corrected the heading but left “is willing allow” unchanged in the sentence.
saved_note: Use “is willing to allow” in headings, but retain “is willing allow” in the body.
